findings.surface: CONC-1 spawn capture disjointness (draft lines 46-57, 'R14 statement-local mint-disjointness verbatim ... no new disjointness machinery ... no new dataflow')
findings.title: Spawn capture loans die at the end of the spawn STATEMENT, so the parent (or a second spawn) freely touches a place a live child holds — the core two-threads-one-location race is accepted
findings.program_or_defect: R14/AMD-1's soundness premise is explicit: 'All mints are consumed by the par statement, which returns only after joining every body' — the mints are call-scoped temporaries (OWN-6) whose statement lasts as long as the bodies run. spawn breaks that premise: the statement returns immediately (own Result<unit, SpawnError>) while the child keeps running, and AMD-1's pseudo-entries are 'every mint of ONE statement', discarded at statement exit. spawn declares no loan clause, R5 issues persist only into let-bound confined holders (spawn's result is an ordinary Result), so NOTHING remains in the loan table after the spawn statement. Three accepted programs: P1 (parent write under child read): `let x: own u64 = 1_u64; scope 'p { let s1: own Result<unit, SpawnError> = spawn['p](reader, &'p x); set x = 2_u64; }` — child's deref(x) races the parent's set; nothing stated rejects the set (the &'p x temp is OWN-6 statement-scoped; the draft never invokes OWN-4 named-region liveness for captures). P2 (cross-spawn &uniq overlap): `scope 'p { let s1 = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p x); let s2 = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p x); }` — two statements, so AMD-1 never compares the mints; loan table empty at the second spawn; two threads hold &uniq to one u64. P3 (post-spawn reborrow): `spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p x); region 'r { let p2: &'r u64 = &'r x; let v: own u64 = deref(p2); }` — parent reads while child writes. What must reject them: a persistent per-capture loan entry (shr for &, uniq for &uniq) on the captured place, held from the spawn statement to scope exit (the join), governing all later parent statements and later spawns via R5/R6. This IS new disjointness machinery — the 'no new dataflow' checker-cost claim is false. The draft's own open-flag sweep (CONC-V2-5) lists H1 lifetime, R15, and the memory model, but NOT this: the parent/child aliasing-in-time gap is unacknowledged. Side defect making the gap visible: by R15's fail-closed letter ('absence of declaration is rejection') spawn, having no declared loan clause, cannot be called at all — the draft is inconsistent between that letter and 'no new machinery'.
findings.minimal_fix: Add to CONC-1: each borrow capture of spawn['p] issues a loan entry on the captured place — kind shr for &'p, kind uniq for &uniq 'p — held by a compiler-introduced scope holder and removed only at scope 'p exit (the join); declare this as spawn's form-table loan clause (satisfying R15's fail-closed letter); state that a capture borrow's liveness is to scope exit (OWN-4 named-region), never an OWN-6 call-scoped temporary. Delete the 'verbatim R14 / no new disjointness machinery / no new dataflow' claims and re-cost the checker.

findings.surface: CONC-2 ordinary-value table, Shareable column for box/buffer/seq/table (draft lines 127-131) + S.3 cq_tx_clone/cq_rx_clone rows (optables.md lines 239-240) vs CQ-3
findings.title: Container Shareable is 'iff deeply immutable (no interior &uniq reachable)' — vacuously true and never consults payload Shareability, so two threads reach & to a never-Shareable endpoint and concurrently run the &-receiver clone op
findings.program_or_defect: R1 makes borrow-typed fields unspellable outside confined types and confined types uninstantiable as container payloads, so 'no interior &uniq reachable' is TRUE for every spellable ordinary container — the column filters nothing. Meanwhile AMD-6 de-confined the cq carriers, and BRAND-1 5.1 explicitly blesses seq<cq_tx<'b1, T>, N>. Program: mint `cq_new<u64, mpmc, 2>()`, push the tx into `sq: own seq<cq_tx<'qa, u64>, 4>`; `fn toucher[brand 'q : mpmc, 'p](sq: &'p seq<cq_tx<'q, u64>, 4>) -> own unit reads('p) { let e: &'p cq_tx<'q, u64> = seq_get(sq, 0_u64); let t2: own cq_tx<'q, u64> = cq_tx_clone(e); ... }` then `scope 'p { spawn['p](toucher, &'p sq); spawn['p](toucher, &'p sq); }`. Both &'p captures pass CONC-2 (referent seq is 'deeply immutable' — vacuous yes; cq_tx's never-Shareable row is never consulted). Both threads hold &'p to ONE endpoint value and concurrently invoke cq_tx_clone — reads('x), writes('q): concurrent writes to the queue's endpoint-registration/count state, on an op that is (a) outside CQ-3's reviewed model ('concurrency is distinct endpoints on distinct threads' — here one endpoint, two threads), (b) outside R15's concurrent-invocation obligation (loan-NONE rows on an arguably non-loanable form), and (c) effect-homeless after 5.5 struck writes('q) (the clone's receiver is SHARED &'x, so the write has no endpoint-borrow-region home). The load-bearing sentence CONC-V2-1 and CONC-2 quote — 'every endpoint op takes &uniq' (CQ-3) — is FALSE for S.3's own clone rows. Same hole through box<cq_tx<'q,T>>, table<K, cq_tx, h>, and user `struct Wrap { tx: cq_tx<'q, u64>; }` (spellable post-AMD-6; user structs/enums, slice, array are absent from the CONC-2 table entirely and no fail-closed default is stated).
findings.minimal_fix: Replace the ordinary Shareable column with 'iff every payload/field Shareable' (a total structural induction over ordinary types including user structs/enums, closed at monomorphization, with an explicit fail-closed clause for unlisted forms — slice, uslice, ahdl: not Sendable/Shareable in v1). Reconcile CQ-3 with the clone rows: either re-mode cq_tx_clone/cq_rx_clone to &uniq receivers or add both rows to the R15 concurrent-invocation obligation set and restore an effect home for their queue-state write.

findings.surface: CONC-3 guard_uniq interior view ([CONC-V2-4] 'Interior view (R6/R3)' clause; Walk 3 Direction A)
findings.title: The guard's &uniq interior view is booked at the MUTEX's region 'm with no loan entry tying it to the guard — a self-tied helper returns it past the unlock, yielding two live &uniq to one location and a lock-free write racing another thread's locked write
findings.program_or_defect: guard_uniq declares no loan clause (R15's letter would reject every call; the walks presuppose let-binding), and the clause it cites — R3 statement-scoping, 'consumed by its receiving call' — is unusable under GRAM-9 ANF (a call result is not an atom, so a strictly statement-scoped result can never be an argument; Walk 3's 'works' already uses the let-binding reading). Under that reading: `fn peek['m, 'x](g: &'x guard<'m, u64>) -> &uniq 'm u64 reads('x) { let r: &uniq 'm u64 = guard_uniq(&'x g); return r; }` — R9 legalizes the declaration ('m self-ties to the borrow-mode g; self-tie is rejected only for own-mode params or results), R10(b) issues entries only for CONFINED results so r carries no entry, and 'm is a fn region parameter so the return is legal. Caller: `region 'm { let mr: &'m mutex<u64> = &'m mx; let p: &uniq 'm u64 = match cond { True() => { let g: own guard<'m, u64> = mtx_lock(mr); let q: &uniq 'm u64 = peek(&'x g); give q; } False() => { ... } } set deref(p) = 7_u64; }` — GIVE-1 judges the borrow give exactly as a returned borrow ('m = 'm, legal); at arm scope end g is auto-consumed (R7) and the UNLOCK runs — no check fires because no live entry overlaps g (the view holds none). p then writes the interior LOCK-FREE while a child thread (mutex<u64> is Shareable, &'p-captured per Walk 3A) holds the lock and writes under it: two threads, one location, unsynchronized. Even single-threaded, `let g2 = mtx_lock(mr)` after the match gives a second live &uniq beside p — the OWN-9 noalias fact channel is broken before any thread exists. The R7 reverse-order argument saves only the same-block case; the cross-function/give path has no stated defense.
findings.minimal_fix: Give guard_uniq a declared loan clause: ISSUE &uniq (interior) with source the GUARD holder's place — entry (g, uniq, view) — so R7's overlapped-drop check blocks unlock under a live view; and give it a DEDICATED result region 'v (the pool_entry pattern), never 'm, so the view is confined to a sub-block of the guard's life and can never be returned or given past it. State explicitly that mtx_lock/guard_uniq/guard-drop rows are R15 fail-closed until their loan clauses are written.

findings.surface: CONC-1 publication semantics (no clause; open-flag sweep item 2 lists only CQ-6 and the mutex)
findings.title: spawn and scope-exit join are nowhere stated to be release/acquire (happens-before) edges — every borrow- or move-capture handoff's race-freedom has no normative basis
findings.program_or_defect: Program: `let x: own u64 = 0_u64; set x = 42_u64; scope 'p { spawn['p](reader, &'p x); }` — the parent's plain pre-spawn write is read by the child; and conversely a child's writes through a &uniq 'p capture are read by the parent after scope exit. No CONC rule makes spawn (parent-at-spawn happens-before body entry) or join (body exit happens-before scope-exit return) synchronization edges; CQ-6 covers only queue handoffs and R15's lock covers only the mutex. Open-flag 2 concedes the memory model is unaxiomatized but omits spawn/join from the list of contracts to pin — so even the planned review would not pin the edge that every scoped-thread program rides on. D1 declares the race impossible; for capture publication nothing in the delta makes that true.
findings.minimal_fix: Add one normative clause to CONC-1: the spawn statement is a release edge from the parent to child body entry, and each child's body exit is a release edge acquired by the scope-exit join (and by a returning-Err spawn for the never-ran case); add spawn/join to open-flag 2's memory-model pin list.

findings.surface: CONC-3 mtx_lock/unlock effect rows (absent) vs R14 slot-mode check and EFF-3; CONC-1 scope effect row ('each body's declared row restricted to 'p and to heap')
findings.title: mtx_lock has no effect row, and every candidate row breaks something: writes('m) makes R14's 'no writes through replicated slots' reject Walk 3A (the delta's canonical ACCEPT); pure licenses EFF-3 dedup of two locks (aliased guards/double unlock); reads('m) hides a real mutation with no stated reordering pin
findings.program_or_defect: Delta 1 states mtx_lock's signature and loan clause but no effects column anywhere. Walk 3 Direction A has each par body receive a replicate-shared &'p mutex<u64> and call mtx_lock inside — the lock word IS written through that shared borrow, so an honest writes('m) row makes R14's slot-mode check ('no writes through replicated slots') reject the delta's own canonical pattern; a pure row lets EFF-3 deduplicate two `mtx_lock(mr)` calls with equal arguments into one guard consumed twice; a reads('m) row (the only consistent choice) rows a mutation as a read with no stated rule that sync ops are reorder-opaque — and the 5.5 precedent (writes('q) struck from all cq rows in favor of writes('e)) shows the effect grammar currently has NO honest spelling for cross-thread sync-cell mutation reached through a shared receiver. Compounding: CONC-1's 'each body's declared row restricted to 'p and to heap' reads as dropping a child's effects on OUTER regions (a body locking an outer-region mutex) from the scope's row, hiding them from the enclosing function's callers (OWN-12).
findings.minimal_fix: State the rows: mtx_lock reads('m) [blocks, pending BLOCKS-EFFECT], unlock as the R7 drop's form-declared release; add one clause that R14's no-writes-through-replicated-slots refers to loan-judged source writes and that R15-certified sync internals are the reviewed exception; pin that no optimizer license (EFF-3 or otherwise) attaches to reads-rows of R15 sync ops; rewrite the scope row clause to 'p-effects confined at scope exit, all outer-region effects propagate.

findings.surface: CONC-2 loan-token class row ('This is a derivation, not a stipulation: R2 ... forbids storing or sending') vs R2's call-argument allowance and CONC-V2-1's captures-are-arguments framing
findings.title: The never-Sendable-for-loan-tokens 'derivation' from R2 fails textually: R2 explicitly permits a confined value as a call argument, and the draft itself says a capture is 'an ordinary carrier ARGUMENT of the spawn body's fn'
findings.program_or_defect: Walk 2 asserts a spawn capture 'stores it into the child's env, none of those [R2 positions]' — but CONC-1/CONC-V2-1 define captures as arguments of a named fn (R14 slots even accept holder mints), and R2's legal positions include 'a call argument (by own or by a statement-scoped minted borrow)'. So `spawn['p](worker, move g)` with g: own guard<'m, u64> is, by the letter, an R2-legal argument position, and the promised derivation does not reject it; only the stipulated class row ('never Sendable, never Shareable') does. The conclusion is right; the claimed ground is not, and a checker built from the derivation as written would accept the guard capture.
findings.minimal_fix: Add one sentence making it normative rather than derived: a spawn capture position is NOT an R2 call-argument position (spawn is not a call for R2's purposes); any capture whose type is opaque-confined rejects at the capture citing CONC-2.

findings.surface: CONC-1 loop interaction ('a spawn inside a loop body may capture only regions introduced inside the loop body') + kernel OWN-11 vs the D16 admission rationale (concurrent 51-map scenarios 11/38/41/42/44)
findings.title: Loop-spawned workers cannot capture &'p/&uniq 'p at all (OWN-11 forbids naming outer regions in a loop), so N shared-state workers exist only by syntactic unrolling — the admission story overstates spellable coverage, and any future carve-out re-opens finding 1
findings.program_or_defect: `scope 'p { loop @l { let s = spawn['p](worker, &'p shared); ... } }` — OWN-11 ('a borrow_expr may only name regions introduced inside @l's body') rejects the &'p capture, and CONC-1's own clause confirms only loop-local regions are capturable, i.e. loop-spawned children can share nothing of the parent. Not a race (it is conservative), but the named consumers are multi-worker scenarios, and the only spelling is a constant number of unrolled spawn statements. Any carve-out admitting &'p captures per iteration is exactly finding 1's cross-iteration &uniq overlap (two iterations' children capturing one place) and must land only together with the persistent capture-loan machinery.
findings.minimal_fix: Record the restriction as intended for this delta (loop spawns take own captures only) in CONC-1 and the admission text, and register the carve-out as a future delta explicitly gated on finding 1's persistent capture-loan entries.

lens: DATA-RACE SOUNDNESS — construct programs placing two threads in unsynchronized access to one location, across CONC-1 scoped threads, CONC-2 Send/Share, CONC-3 mutex/guard, CONC-4 trap/abort, against RULES-RATIFIED R1-R15/AMD-1..6, kernel-spec-v0.6 (CAP-1, OWN, EFF, SCOPE-4/EFF-4), and optables S.3
verdict: NOT ADOPTABLE AS DRAFTED. Three FATALs, each a checker-accepted two-thread unsynchronized access under the draft's stated or intended readings: (1) spawn capture loans evaporate at statement end because the delta reuses R14/AMD-1 statement-local machinery whose soundness premise (the statement blocks until join) spawn does not satisfy — parent-write-under-child-read, cross-spawn &uniq overlap, and post-spawn reborrow all accept; (2) the ordinary-container Shareable column ('deeply immutable') is vacuously true and never consults payload Shareability, letting two threads reach & to a never-Shareable endpoint and concurrently run the &-receiver clone rows that themselves falsify CQ-3's 'every endpoint op takes &uniq'; (3) the guard's interior view has no loan clause and, under the let-binding reading the delta's own walks use, escapes past the unlock via an R9 self-tied helper plus GIVE-1, yielding two live &uniq to one location single-threaded and a lock-free write racing a locked writer cross-thread. Two MAJORs: spawn/join are nowhere declared happens-before edges (capture publication has no normative basis, and open-flag 2's pin list omits them), and mtx_lock's missing effect row is inconsistent under every candidate completion (writes('m) rejects Walk 3A via R14; pure invites EFF-3 lock dedup; reads('m) is an unpinned lie), with the scope row clause additionally dropping outer-region effects. The draft's honest open flags (R15 concurrent-invocation trust, memory model) are necessary but NOT sufficient — every hole above is on the checker side the draft claims closed, and none appears in its own CONC-V2-5 sweep. Repair all five, then full re-attack before any adoption; per the standing rule, this review must be re-run on the repaired text, since each fix (spawn's loan clause, the Shareable induction, guard_uniq's issue clause) is itself new fact-channel surface.
hardest_failed: Slice smuggle: own-capture `move s` of slice<'r, u8> (a borrow in value clothing, bypassing the &'p-capture Shareable check) into a child, then parent `set index<u8>(b, 0_u64)` — DEFEATED: kernel liveness is region-extent, not binding-based (OWN-4), so the slice's shared freeze on b holds for all of 'r no matter which thread holds the value; no parent write is reachable while the child can read. (The CONC-2 table's silence on slice remains folded into finding 2's totality fix.)

mutex<cq_rx<'q, u64>> shared &'p across threads as a transfer cell for a never-Shareable endpoint — SOUND: Shareable-iff-T-Sendable is exactly the lock-serialization contract; the endpoint state crosses only under the lock's release/acquire, which is what Sendable licenses.

Trap window: child traps while siblings execute during the abort's real (non-instantaneous) teardown window — DEFEATED: a trapping op performs no write (SCOPE-4/EFF-4), pre-trap history was race-free, so surviving threads observe only a state indistinguishable from a slow child; worst case is blocking on a never-unlocked mutex inside a dying process (liveness, not a race).

Forging two spsc producers onto two threads: clone rows are a compile-time reject for spsc (CQ-3), QueueEnds destructure yields exactly one tx (CQ-4/OWN-13 whole-binding death), and container transit is blocked by TYPE-5 brand equality (BRAND-1 5.1) — no spelling found.

Guard into a par split-unique slot (&uniq mint of the guard holder per R3/R6 sole-exception): the par statement suspends the parent and AMD-1 makes the slot disjoint from every other mint, so exactly one live toucher of the interior exists for the statement's extent — no cross-thread coexistence.

Same-thread relock for aliased guards: in-function, R5's uniq-issue overlap check rejects the second mtx_lock under the first guard's live interior entry; cross-function the fresh per-function loan table admits it but the runtime lock makes it a self-deadlock — a liveness failure outside D1, not a race.

findings.surface: RULES-RATIFIED.md R15 + KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md [CONC-3]/(H3) [CONC-V2-4] (m2-spec-mass/KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md, m1-loan-judgment/RULES-RATIFIED.md)
findings.title: R15's 'safe under concurrent invocation' is a per-invocation predicate and cannot carry mutex exclusivity; a conforming-but-racy lock passes the stated obligation
findings.program_or_defect: R15 obliges only that a &-receiver op's internals 'preserve every interior address and are safe under concurrent invocation'. Mutual exclusion is a CROSS-invocation, liveness-scoped, thread-agnostic global invariant on the set of live issued tokens — nowhere stated. Two sealed implementations satisfy the literal text and admit races the checker accepts: (a) recursive lock: `fn helper['m](m: &'m mutex<u64>) -> own unit { let g2 = mtx_lock(m); ... }` called as `let g1 = mtx_lock(&'m m); helper(&'m m);` — the & mint of the root is legal under the interior-uniq entry (R6 restricts only move/rebind/assign/own/drop on overlapping places; R5's overlap check lives in the caller's table, helper's table is empty per R10c), so a recursive acquire SUCCEEDS and one thread holds two live guards = two aliased `&uniq 'm u64` (noalias fact violated, miscompile); (b) reader-shared acquire (rwlock-flavored mtx_lock): Walk 3 hands two threads concurrent guards — each invocation is individually 'safe'. Note R14's no-writes-through-replicated-slots is NO backstop: the body's interior writes ride a body-local region that CAT-5a(ii) confines out of the row, so the effect check is vacuous for synchronizers; everything rides on the unstated obligation.
findings.minimal_fix: Amend R15 (or add CONC-3 clause R15-X) stating the exact sealed-author proof for any `issues uniq` op on a shared receiver: (O1) tenure exclusion — at most one issued token live PROCESS-WIDE per receiver instance at every instant, tenure = op-return to R7-release completion, thread-agnostic (outlaws recursive success; same-thread re-acquire must block or trap, never issue); (O2) handoff HB — release_i synchronizes-with issue-return_{i+1}; (O3) interior address stability (already present); (O4) no token issued on any non-acquiring path (try/timeout variants issue only on the Ok arm). Require the hostile review to discharge O1-O4 per form.

findings.surface: kernel-spec-v0.6.md CAP-1 + optables.md CQ-6 + KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md Delta-1 open flag 2
findings.title: D1 race-freedom is asserted, not checkable: the kernel defines no data race, no happens-before, no synchronizes-with; even the flagship walks' correctness is unstatable
findings.program_or_defect: CAP-1 declares 'Data-race impossibility is D1 law'; CQ-6 says 'Every handoff is release/acquire: reads of a received T are data-race-free (D1)' — but the kernel has no definition of conflicting access, race, HB, or what release/acquire normatively means (open flag 2 admits it). Concretely unprovable today: Walk 1's parent consuming `got` after `scope 'p` exit, and any R14 par where the parent reads split-view `&uniq` writes after join — the child-writes-before-parent-reads ordering rests on a JOIN edge no kernel clause states; likewise the spawn-capture publication edge. For the sealed forms D1 is currently a per-form axiom with no reduction target.
findings.minimal_fix: Add a ~10-clause kernel memory-model statement (the reduction target for every sealed proof): MM-0 definitions — location, conflicting accesses, data race (conflicting, unordered by HB), HB = program order plus the closed synchronizes-with edge list, D1 restated as the theorem 'accepted program implies every conflicting pair is HB-ordered on language-managed memory', scoped per SCOPE-3 (FFI, external writers of RO maps, TCB internals excluded; mapped bytes carry no facts); MM-1 spawn edge (parent's pre-spawn actions HB child start — capture publication); MM-2 join edge (each child's last action HB scope/par exit); MM-3 mutex tenure order (O1-O4 of finding 1); MM-4 queue handoff (cq_send of item v synchronizes-with the cq_recv returning v; one publish per batch covers its items; last-tx-drop HB RecvClosed, last-rx-drop HB QueueClosed(value) handback); MM-5 endpoint seriality (all ops on one endpoint HB-totally-ordered — via affinity + &uniq receivers + MM-1/2/3 on every transfer path; sealed proofs may assume HB-seriality and spsc two-endpoint totality, never thread identity); MM-6 reclamation (every op/drop on any endpoint HB the storage free in the last drop; free unique; acq_rel counting — the Arc-drop shape); MM-7 sealed-internal effect opacity (finding 5); MM-8 split views byte-disjoint; MM-9 trap precedes the violating effect, abort path writes no language-visible state; MM-10 normative mapping of release/acquire to C11/LLVM orderings with per-target proofs, arm64 as the honest bed.

findings.surface: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md [CONC-1] interactions paragraph + kernel-spec-v0.6.md OWN-4/OWN-6/OWN-12
findings.title: Spawn-capture borrows have statement-scoped liveness under the cited machinery, so a two-statement program races: cross-spawn and spawn-vs-parent exclusivity has no carrier
findings.program_or_defect: CONC-1 claims 'the cross-capture disjointness of a scope is R14's statement-local mint-disjointness verbatim ... no new disjointness machinery'. AMD-1 pseudo-entries are per-STATEMENT; R14 is sound only because par joins within its one statement. spawn returns immediately, and per OWN-6 an unbound argument borrow is 'a call-scoped temporary, live until the end of the enclosing statement' (OWN-12: live for the duration of the call — which returns at once). So: `scope 'p { let s1 = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p buf); let s2 = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p buf); }` — statement 2 sees no live overlapping borrow; both children write buf concurrently. Simpler still: `scope 'p { let s1 = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p buf); set buf[0] = 1_u64; }` — parent writes while the child writes. (H1) flags only lifetime ('no borrow outlives 'p'), never cross-statement aliasing.
findings.minimal_fix: State the capture-extent rule explicitly: each spawn capture borrow is held until the scope's join point — either OWN-4 named-region liveness governs spawn captures (live to end of 'p, overriding OWN-6/OWN-12 for spawn arguments, so OWN-5 rejects the second &uniq and the parent's write), or spawn is a form op issuing a loan entry per capture held by the scope statement and removed at scope exit. Add the two-spawn and spawn-then-parent-write programs as REJECT walks; retract the 'no new disjointness machinery' claim.

findings.surface: RULES-RATIFIED.md R15 scope wording + KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md 5.5 [AMD-6] + optables.md S.3 CQ-5/cq_tx_clone
findings.title: Post-AMD-6 the conc_queue sits outside R15's obligation net: the cross-thread teardown (last-drop free), clone rows, and all cq ops carry no stated concurrency proof obligation
findings.program_or_defect: R15's concurrent-invocation obligation attaches only to '& receiver mode ... on a loanable form'. AMD-6 reclassifies cq endpoints as brand-carriers with loan NONE, so conc_queue is not a loanable form; cq_send/cq_recv take &uniq receivers anyway; endpoint drops are not holder drops (no entries), so R15's 'release actions run by holder drops' clause does not reach the CQ-5 drain-on-drop protocol. Yet CQ-5's counting free is the language's one CROSS-THREAD free: two endpoint drops racing on distinct threads (parent drops rx as child drops tx) double-free the slot storage unless the sealed counting is acq_rel-fenced — and no rule text demands that proof. cq_tx_clone (& receiver, mpmc, concurrent with sends on peer endpoints) likewise carries no obligation. A hostile-review model built strictly from R15's text would rule the queue out of scope — the most dangerous form would ship unreviewed. B1V22-2 asserts the storage obligation 'under R15's hostile review', but R15's own scoping words exclude it.
findings.minimal_fix: Restate the obligation attach point by BEHAVIOR, not loan class: every sealed op or compiler-derived drop whose body may access state reachable from another live binding or thread (cross-invocation-shared internal state) carries the concurrent-invocation, publication (MM-4/5), and reclamation (MM-6) obligations — explicitly including compiler-derived endpoint drops and clone rows. Enumerate the CONC review's mandatory scope: mtx_lock/guard drop, all ten cq rows, both clone rows, endpoint drop bodies, cq_new.

findings.surface: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md 5.5 effect-row amendment ('reads('q)/writes('q) STRUCK') + optables.md CAT-5a(ii) + S.3 rows
findings.title: Brand-effect erasure plus internal-region confinement makes queue mutation effect-invisible: a pure-rowed enqueue is derivable, a false optimizer fact in single-threaded code
findings.program_or_defect: 5.5 mandates a queue op's only named effect be its ENDPOINT'S BORROW REGION ('cq_send ... exhibit writes('e)'), and CAT-5a(ii) says effects confined to an internally minted region do not escape the fn's row. Compose them: `fn send_one(tx: own cq_tx<'q, u64>, v: own u64) -> own cq_tx<'q, u64> { region 'a { let s = cq_try_send(&uniq 'a tx, v); } return move tx; }` — the send's sole effect writes('a) is confined, cq_try_send neither allocates nor traps, so send_one's derived row is PURE while it publishes an item another thread's rx will observe. Effect rows are optimizer facts in this project; a pure-labeled enqueue may be reordered or treated as observationally inert — a miscompile of a correct program, no concurrency needed. Two distinct-region sequential borrows of tx and rx in one fn similarly yield disjoint-region rows for ops that mutate one shared heap block (false disjointness).
findings.minimal_fix: Kernel clause MM-7: sealed-form shared internal state is effect-opaque — every op touching it carries a non-erasable effect atom that survives region confinement (e.g. a `sync` effect word, or attribute the effect to the endpoint's owning binding rather than the borrow region); no purity, CSE, reordering, or region-disjointness fact may be derived across sealed-internal state from effect rows. Apply to all sealed sync ops (cq rows, mtx_lock, guard drop/unlock) and rewrite the S.3 rows accordingly in the same landing as the 5.5 strike.

findings.surface: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md [CONC-2] ordinary-form table (Shareable column) vs mutex row
findings.title: The Shareable rule 'iff deeply immutable (no interior &uniq reachable)' is undefined vocabulary and contradicts the mutex row on containers-of-synchronizers
findings.program_or_defect: `seq<mutex<u64>, 4>` (striped counters, the scenario-40 shape): via mtx_lock an interior &uniq IS reachable through the container, so the container row derives NOT Shareable — while `mutex<u64>` alone is Shareable. The same par/scope program accepts when the mutex is bare and rejects when it is an element: replicate-& slot of the stripe array fires the container row's 'deeply immutable' clause. 'Deeply immutable' has no kernel definition, and by AMD-2 every &-shared ordinary value is already transitively unwritable, making the clause either vacuous or (as here) wrongly exclusive of exactly the sealed-synchronizer payloads that motivate sharing.
findings.minimal_fix: Make Shareable compositional: primitives/tag-only yes; box/buffer/seq/table Shareable iff every payload Shareable; mutex<T> Shareable iff T Sendable (explicit row, finding 7); delete 'deeply immutable' or define it as 'no payload whose capability row withholds Shareable'. Add the seq<mutex<u64>,4> striped-lock walk as an ACCEPT regression.

findings.surface: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md [CONC-2] mutex row + open flag 3; member audit has no mutex capability row
findings.title: mutex<T> Send/Share is a per-form trusted stipulation mislabeled as a derivation; the missing explicit capability row is a real gap
findings.program_or_defect: CONC-2's headline is 'DERIVED, not asserted per form', but the ordinary-value rule would derive Shareable = NO for mutex (interior &uniq reachable); the actual justification — serialization makes &-sharing need only T Sendable — is precisely the sealed exclusion property (finding 1's O1/O2) that only the trusted proof supplies. The content matches the standard judgment (Sendable iff T Sendable; Shareable iff T Sendable, never requiring T Shareable — correct), but it is a third class, not a table read-off, and the audit has no normative row for the review to attack. Corollary needing the row's justification pointer: mutex<cq_tx<'q,T>> is Shareable, so serialized MULTI-thread use of one spsc endpoint is legal — sound only under MM-5's HB-seriality reading, not CQ-3's literal 'single-threaded by construction'.
findings.minimal_fix: Land the explicit capability row: 'mutex<T>: Sendable iff T Sendable; Shareable iff T Sendable — justified solely by the CONC-3 tenure-exclusion obligation, never by structure', as a named third declaration class (sealed synchronizer) in AMD-6's taxonomy; retitle CONC-2's claim to 'derived for loan-token/brand-carrier/ordinary classes; stipulated-with-obligation for synchronizers'.

findings.surface: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md [CONC-4] steps 2-4 + kernel-spec-v0.6.md SCOPE-4/EFF-4 + MEMBER-AUDIT-THREADS-IO.md (signal_term_flag, pwrite/WAL, map rows)
findings.title: CONC-4's abort argument rests on false instantaneity and an unscoped inertness claim; three premises it needs are unstated
findings.program_or_defect: (i) 'the joining parent thread is itself torn down at the same instant' is false on both pinned targets: abort delivers SIGABRT to the trapping thread; siblings run concurrently for an unbounded scheduling window. The conclusion survives only via two unstated premises: a trap precedes every effect of the violating operation (stated per-op for OP-9, never as a general SCOPE-4 clause), and the abort/report path writes no language-visible state. (ii) 'an unflushed buffer — none is reachable after a process-wide abort, so leaving them is observationally inert' overclaims: in-flight pwrite/net_sendv issued before the trap land after it, and a sibling mid-syscall can complete a partial write during the window — the WAL/peer observes a torn prefix (the exact fault-injection case; the KV recovery path is the consumer). Not a D1 hole, but as written it licenses skipping the abort-fault-injection battery. (iii) The no-in-process-observer premise silently depends on the sealed signal invariant (sticky-flag handler only, signals_general deferred with 'never user handlers') — the runtime's own handler DOES run during the window; it is benign only by that invariant. (Cross-process mmap observers are genuinely closed: shared writable mappings are a ratified v1 non-goal; posix_spawn's brief CLONE_VM share is TCB.)
findings.minimal_fix: Reword CONC-4 (2)-(4): add the general SCOPE-4/EFF-4 clause 'a trap happens-before any effect of the violating operation; the abort path performs no language-visible write'; replace 'same instant' with 'no thread ever returns from a join or observes a trap as a value; surviving threads observe only pre-trap-valid state during the teardown window'; scope inertness to process memory and add 'external state receives power-loss-prefix semantics' with a named abort-mid-scenario fault-injection battery; cite the no-user-signal-handler invariant as a load-bearing premise; scope the claim to the pinned OS targets.

findings.surface: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md [CONC-2] loan-token bullet + Walk 2 vs [CONC-V2-1]; RULES-RATIFIED.md R2
findings.title: The guard never-Sendable 'derivation' from R2 equivocates on what a spawn capture is
findings.program_or_defect: R2 explicitly permits a confined value as 'a call argument'. CONC-V2-1 models a spawn capture as 'an ordinary carrier ARGUMENT of the spawn body's fn' (for endpoints); Walk 2 rejects the guard capture by calling the same position 'stores it into the child's env, none of those'. Both readings cannot hold; under the argument model R2 does not reject `spawn['p](worker, move g)`, so the (correct) reject is a new CONC-2 stipulation, not an R2 corollary — and CONC-2's 'this is a derivation, not a stipulation' is wrong for its first bullet.
findings.minimal_fix: One clause: 'a spawn capture is a thread-boundary position; the opaque-confined loan-token class is rejected there in every capture mode — a CONC-2 rule proper (new authority under this review), not an R2 consequence'; or amend R2's forbidden list with 'spawn/par capture'. Keep the R6 second-thread-shares-the-exclusive-interior argument as the independent Shareable bar.

findings.surface: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md [CONC-3]: absent mutex op-table rows; optables.md CQ-7 [blocks]; RULES-RATIFIED.md R14 effect discipline
findings.title: mtx_lock/guard_uniq have no op-table rows: no effect row, no [blocks] mark, no failure column, no CG pin — so Walk 3 is not effect-checkable as drafted
findings.program_or_defect: The delta defines mtx_lock/guard_uniq only in prose. mtx_lock blocks (needs CQ-7's [blocks] pending flag), mutates the lock word through a & receiver (its effect accounting must be reconciled with R14's 'no writes through replicated slots' for the canonical Walk 3 — currently reconcilable only by the body-local-region confinement that finding 5 shows is itself a defect), has no stated try/timeout variants (O4 needs the only-issue-on-Ok discipline named), and no CG-LOCK pin. The hostile review has no normative rows to attack for the single most dangerous form.
findings.minimal_fix: Land S.4-style rows before the review: mtx_new, mtx_lock (`['m](m: &'m mutex<T>) -> own guard<'m, T>`, loan `issues uniq` on interior, effects `sync [blocks]` per MM-7, failure `—`, cg CG-LOCK with the acquire/release lowering pinned), guard_uniq (R3 statement-scoped), guard drop (R7 release = unlock, trap-free body per open flag 4), optional mtx_try_lock returning Result issuing only on Ok.

findings.surface: optables.md CQ-3 wording ('per-endpoint use is single-threaded by construction') + KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md CONC-V2-1
findings.title: Endpoint seriality must be specified as HB-total-order, not thread affinity: mutex<cq_tx> legally migrates one endpoint across threads
findings.program_or_defect: Since mutex<T> is Shareable iff T Sendable and cq_tx is Sendable, `mutex<cq_tx<'q, u64>>` shared &'p across a scope lets many threads serially lock and send on one spsc producer endpoint — accepted, and sound only because mutex tenures chain HB edges (MM-3). A sealed spsc implementation that reads CQ-3 literally and assumes thread identity (thread-local cursor caching keyed by thread id, biased/asymmetric fences) is broken by this accepted program.
findings.minimal_fix: Reword CQ-3/CONC assumptions to 'all operations on one endpoint are HB-totally-ordered (MM-5); implementations must not assume thread identity'; add mutex<cq_tx> serialized-send as a review walk.

lens: THE R15 TRUSTED OBLIGATION AND THE MEMORY MODEL: whether R15's '&-receiver ops safe under concurrent invocation' can carry mutex cross-thread exclusivity; whether D1 race-freedom is checkable without an axiomatized happens-before model under CQ-6's release/acquire; whether CONC-4's trap-is-abort no-observer argument holds on real targets; and whether the inferred mutex<T> Send/Share row is sound.
verdict: BLOCK adoption of Delta 1 as drafted. The architecture is the right shape — static capabilities, a closed set of sealed synchronization points, trap = abort — but the foundation the lens targeted does not hold: (1) R15 as written is a NAME for an unstated proof; a sealed author must today prove only per-invocation safety, and two conforming implementations (recursive lock via the cross-function re-lock program; reader-shared acquire) yield checker-accepted data races/aliased &uniq. (2) D1 is currently ASSERTED for the CONC forms: with no kernel definition of race, happens-before, or release/acquire, even the flagship walks' parent-reads-after-join correctness is unstatable, and every sealed proof lacks a reduction target. (3) Two further textual holes admit races/double-frees outright: spawn-capture borrows are statement-scoped temporaries under the cited machinery (two-line accepted race), and post-AMD-6 the conc_queue — including the cross-thread last-drop free and clone rows — sits entirely outside R15's obligation net. (4) Brand-effect erasure composed with region confinement derives a pure-rowed enqueue: a false optimizer fact without any concurrency. CONC-4's conclusion survives but its argument does not (false instantaneity; inertness unscoped to process memory; the sealed-signal-handler premise unnamed); mutex Send/Share is correct in content but is a stipulation riding the unstated exclusion proof, not a derivation, and the structural Shareable rule contradicts it on seq<mutex<T>>. THE EXACT OBLIGATION LIST THE CONC HOSTILE-REVIEW MODEL MUST DISCHARGE (the minimal kernel memory-model statement, MM-0..MM-10): MM-0 definitions and theorem — byte locations; conflicting accesses; data race = conflicting, cross-thread, not HB-ordered; HB = program order plus the closed synchronizes-with list below; D1 restated: an accepted program has no data race on language-managed memory, scoped by SCOPE-3 (gated FFI, external writers of RO-mapped files, and TCB internals excluded; mapped bytes never carry facts). MM-1 spawn/publication edge: everything sequenced-before spawn['p] in the parent happens-before the child body's first action (capture initialization is published). MM-2 join edge: each child's last action happens-before scope-'p exit (and par-statement return) — required for split-view &uniq writes read by the parent. MM-3 mutex tenure order: per instance, a total HB order of guard tenures with O1 process-wide tenure exclusion (thread-agnostic — recursive acquire must never issue), O2 release_i synchronizes-with lock-return_{i+1}, O3 interior address stability, O4 token issued only on acquiring paths. MM-4 queue publication: cq_send(v) synchronizes-with the cq_recv returning v; one release publish per batch covers its items; last-tx-drop HB any RecvClosed observation; last-rx-drop HB any QueueClosed(value) ownership handback. MM-5 endpoint single-owner property, stated soundly: all ops on one endpoint are HB-totally-ordered (from affinity + &uniq receivers + MM-1/2/3 edges on every legal transfer, including mutex<cq_tx> migration); spsc additionally has exactly-two-endpoint totality (clone rows reject); sealed proofs may assume HB-seriality, never thread identity. MM-6 reclamation/epoch safety: every op and drop on any endpoint happens-before the storage free in the last endpoint's drop; the free is unique (exact acq_rel counting, the Arc-drop shape); the freeing thread's remaining-item drops see all published items via MM-4. MM-7 non-interference/effect opacity: sealed sync ops are never pure; a non-erasable effect atom survives region confinement; no region-disjointness, CSE, DSE, or code motion across sealed-internal shared state or against the acquire/release direction; requires-engine facts over shared-reachable state fail closed (generalizing CQ-6's no-occupancy-facts rule). MM-8 par/scope split views partition into byte-disjoint ranges. MM-9 trap ordering: a trap happens-before any effect of the violating operation; the abort path writes no language-visible state; surviving threads observe only pre-trap-valid in-process state during the teardown window; external state gets power-loss-prefix semantics with a mandatory abort-mid-scenario fault-injection battery. MM-10 target mapping: release/acquire/acq_rel pinned to C11/LLVM orderings with per-target lowering proofs; arm64 is the honest stress bed (Chase-Lev/Vyukov fence audits under that mapping). Adopt only after MM-0..MM-10 land as kernel text, R15 gains the O1-O4 tenure-exclusion schema with behavior-based (not loan-class-based) attachment covering cq rows, clone rows, and derived drops, the spawn-capture liveness rule and mutex op rows are written, and the walks (two-spawn REJECT, spawn-then-parent-write REJECT, seq<mutex> ACCEPT, mutex<cq_tx> serialized-send, cross-function re-lock) are added to the regression appendix. Reviewed files: /Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/implementation/systems-performance-coverage/m2-spec-mass/KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md, /Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/implementation/systems-performance-coverage/m1-loan-judgment/RULES-RATIFIED.md, /Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/spec/kernel-spec-v0.6.md, /Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/implementation/systems-performance-coverage/m2-spec-mass/optables.md, /Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/implementation/systems-performance-coverage/MEMBER-AUDIT-THREADS-IO.md.
hardest_failed: Guard laundering across threads through data paths: tried sending guard<'m,T> as a cq payload, as a container element, and via return — triple-barred (CQ-1 requires T Sendable at instantiation; R1 forbids confined types instantiating any generic or sealed-form type parameter; R13 blocks locally-regioned escape). No route exists; the never-Sendable verdict holds even though its R2 derivation is misstated (finding 9).

Same-function recursive mtx_lock: `let g1 = mtx_lock(&'m m); let g2 = mtx_lock(&'m m);` — statically REJECTED: R5's issue precondition requires no entry overlapping the interior, and g1's uniq entry is on that exact place. The static side held; only the cross-function form (finding 1) penetrates, via R10c's per-function table seeding.

Endpoint identity forgery across the spawn boundary: replayed BRAND-1's forge set in Walk-1 vocabulary (two-queue tx/rx swap at a spawn body, container transit through seq<cq_tx<'b1,T>>, spsc-vs-mpmc ep-forge into a cloning body) — all REJECT at TYPE-5 nominal brand equality, the 5.4 all-carriers tie, or the 5.5 clause-(ii) recorded-bound comparison. The brand foundation held against every cross-thread variant I built.

Trap-observation channel: tried to make a child's trap a recoverable value (spawn Result, scope-exit status, queue-side close observation) — none exists: spawn's Result covers only pre-run EAGAIN, scope exit returns no per-child value, and a trapping child aborts the process before any join returns. CONC-4's no-observation-channel conclusion held under the repaired argument of finding 8.

Sharing the guard by & into a child (`spawn['p](worker, &'p g)`): doubly rejected — CONC-2 loan-token never-Shareable, and independently R6's freeze (the guard holds a live interior-uniq entry; a second thread's & would reach the exclusive interior). Held in both capture modes.

findings.surface: [CONC-1] spawn capture liveness x OWN-6/AMD-1 (KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md ~L36-53)
findings.title: Capture borrows are statement-scoped temporaries under the kernel's own rules, so two spawns (or spawn-then-parent-write) alias a &uniq across threads
findings.program_or_defect: let a: own u64 = 0_u64; scope 'p { let s1 = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p a); let s2 = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p a); } — AMD-1 mint-disjointness is STATEMENT-LOCAL (the draft quotes it 'verbatim'), so the two spawns never see each other; OWN-6 makes an unbound borrow a call-scoped temporary 'live until the end of the enclosing statement', so the first &uniq is dead before statement 2 and both are accepted: two threads hold &uniq to one place. Same hole with spawn then `set a = 1_u64;` in the parent. R14 par is safe only because the par STATEMENT blocks until join; spawn removes exactly that premise and the draft adds no replacement rule — it never even chooses between the conflicting OWN-4 (region-liveness) and OWN-6 (statement-temporary) readings.
findings.minimal_fix: Add one rule: each spawn capture borrow becomes a loan-table pseudo-entry (uniq for &uniq, shr for &) on its resolved place, held by an implicit child holder, live until the end of 'p; OWN-6's temporary clause is disapplied for spawn captures, and cross-spawn/parent disjointness is checked against these persistent entries (AMD-1 lifted from statement-local to scope-local for spawn).

findings.surface: [CONC-1] scope exit x STOR-3/R7 early-exit edges (~L38-40)
findings.title: return/break out of the scope block is never stated to join children — child keeps a &'p borrow into a dead frame (UAF)
findings.program_or_defect: fn f() -> own unit ... { let a: own u64 = 0_u64; scope 'p { let sp = spawn['p](writer, &uniq 'p a); return unit; } } — STOR-3 says every edge leaving a region block carries drops; the join is NOT a drop of any binding (spawn returns own Result<unit, SpawnError>, a plain value with no drop action), and the draft only says 'scope exit BLOCKS' — R14's wording ('the par statement returns only after joining') is statement-atomic and does not transfer to a multi-statement block with return/break edges. On the hostile reading the return drops a and pops the frame while the child writes it.
findings.minimal_fix: Define scope exit as EVERY control-flow edge leaving the scope block (fallthrough, break to an outer label, return), and state that the join executes on each such edge BEFORE that edge's R7/STOR-3 releases and drops.

findings.surface: [CONC-3] guard_uniq loan clause x R15/AMD-5/GRAM-9 (~L164-166, 201-204)
findings.title: guard_uniq declares no loan clause, its 'statement-scoped [R3]' story is unspellable in ANF, and the spellable form admits two live &uniq views of one interior
findings.program_or_defect: let g: own guard<'m, u64> = mtx_lock(&'m m); let v1: &uniq 'm u64 = guard_uniq(&'x1 g); let v2: &uniq 'm u64 = guard_uniq(&'x2 g); — GRAM-9 forbids a call in atom position, so the result MUST be let-bound, contradicting 'consumed by its receiving call'; a let-bound result at region 'm lives to 'm's end. The result is not a borrow_expr, so OWN-6 assigns it no holder and OWN-5 is blind; the guard's uniq entry sits on m.interior and R6 only polices mints FROM that place, not op results. No clause is drafted, and the obvious one — issues uniq on source(receiver) — is DECLARATION-REJECTED by AMD-5 (uniq result), which the draft itself invokes one paragraph earlier for mtx_lock ('AMD-5 does not bite'). So 'no new loan machinery' is false: the ratified vocabulary cannot express a uniq interior view out of a uniq token at all. Fail-closed (R15) rescues soundness but makes the mutex's only access op uncallable — either way the delta's central claim is wrong.
findings.minimal_fix: Give guard_uniq an explicit, reviewed clause — a new sibling-view clause (uniq view issued on the guard holder, freezing g so a second view and an early guard drop reject via R6/R7), carried as a marked AMD-5 carve-out — and delete the 'no new loan machinery' headline.

findings.surface: [CONC-2] class partition x R1/R2, and Walk 2's firing ground (~L98-144, 372-389)
findings.title: The 'derived, not asserted' claim is false, the partition misses user confined types, and Walk 2's reject ground contradicts Walk 1's capture model
findings.program_or_defect: CONC-V2-1 says a captured endpoint is 'an ordinary carrier ARGUMENT of the spawn body's fn'; under that model R2 explicitly PERMITS a confined value as a call argument, so Walk 2's stated ground ('a spawn capture stores it into the child's env, none of those [R2 positions]') is the opposite capture model — the two walks cannot both be right, and R2 alone rejects nothing under the argument model (a worker fn taking m: &'m mutex<T>, g: own guard<'m, T> passes R9's tie). The guard reject is therefore a STIPULATION of CONC-2, not a derivation. Separately, the partition ('form-table loan token | brand-carrier | every OTHER type is ordinary owned') classifies a USER confined(uniq) struct carrying a &uniq 'p field as 'ordinary owned', for which no Sendable row exists — borrow-typed payloads have no judgment anywhere in CONC-2.
findings.minimal_fix: State the capture mechanism once (capture = argument of the body fn); repartition by R1 confinement — ANY confined type (form-table or user) is never Sendable/Shareable, stated as a stipulated kernel rule at the spawn boundary — and rewrite Walk 2's firing clause to cite that rule, not R2.

findings.surface: [CONC-2] payload-structural table coverage + recursion (~L124-138)
findings.title: No Sendable/Shareable row exists for user structs/enums, array<T,N>, Option/Result, or slice — the judgment does not cover the type system it governs — and the payload walk is not well-founded on recursive types
findings.program_or_defect: struct Pair { a: u64; b: box<u64>; } ... spawn['p](worker, move p) — the table rows are exactly {primitive/tag-only enum, box/buffer/seq/table, mutex}; Pair matches none, so an own capture of any user composite has no defined judgment: fail-closed makes every real program unspellable, improvisation is unreviewed authority in the single most dangerous judgment. And for enum List { Nil(); Cons(value: u64, next: box<List>); } the 'payload-Sendable walk closed at monomorphization ... no search' diverges: Sendable(List) -> Sendable(box<List>) -> Sendable(List).
findings.minimal_fix: Add the structural rows: user struct/enum Sendable iff every field/payload Sendable (likewise Shareable), array<T,N> as for seq, slice per its mode; define the walk as a memoized greatest-fixpoint (assume-on-cycle) over the finite post-monomorphization type graph so recursion through box terminates.

findings.surface: [CONC-2] ordinary-form Shareable predicate (~L130)
findings.title: 'Shareable iff deeply immutable (no interior &uniq reachable)' references machinery that cannot exist in those payloads and is the wrong predicate
findings.program_or_defect: R1 forbids borrow-typed fields in every non-confined type and forbids instantiating containers with confined types, so 'interior &uniq reachable' is unsatisfiable in box/buffer/seq/table payloads — the condition is vacuous as written, and 'deeply immutable' is not a kernel judgment. Hostile reading: seq<u64, N> is mutable data, hence never Shareable, killing every read-parallel &'p capture (parallel sum over a seq); charitable reading is checker improvisation.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace with: an ordinary form is Shareable iff every payload is Shareable (sound because these forms have no interior mutability under &, OWN-5 read-only; mutex/conc_queue are the interior-mutability exceptions and carry their own rows).

findings.surface: [CONC-1] scope effect row x EFF-2/OWN-10 (~L41-44)
findings.title: 'body's row restricted to 'p and to heap' contradicts EFF-2 for captures rooted at borrow parameters
findings.program_or_defect: OWN-10's borrow-rooted clause ('for p rooted at a borrow of region 'b: 'b must outlive-or-equals 'a') admits a capture &uniq 'p deref(xb) where xb: &uniq 'b u64 is a fn parameter — the child then writes caller storage, the enclosing fn syntactically exhibits writes('b) (EFF-2), but the drafted restriction clause forbids declaring it. Every such program is either wrongly-rowed (rows are optimizer facts — a hidden cross-thread write channel) or rejected by EFF-2's both-ways check, making the clause self-contradictory. EFF-1's grammar also cannot even spell a row 'restricted to 'p' in the fn signature ('p is not in scope there).
findings.minimal_fix: The scope statement exhibits the UNION of each body's row instantiated at the actual capture regions, dropping only regions introduced inside the scope/body (the CAT-5a(ii) discipline); effects on caller-supplied or enclosing regions are never dropped.

findings.surface: [CONC-3] mutex formation rows x R15 fail-closed (~L153-229)
findings.title: No mtx_new, no storage class, no effects/teardown rows — under R15 fail-closed the mutex is unconstructible, so Walks 2 and 3 type-check a value that cannot exist
findings.program_or_defect: CONC-3 drafts exactly two ops (mtx_lock, guard_uniq) and an informal drop-release sentence. There is no constructor row, no Sendable-at-instantiation condition (the CQ-1 analog Walk 3 Direction B silently presumes formable), no effect rows, no S-section. R15: absence of declaration is rejection.
findings.minimal_fix: Draft the full sealed-form section (mtx_new with effects/storage, mtx_lock, guard_uniq, guard drop-release row, capability row), the same nine-column discipline as S.3, in the same delta.

findings.surface: [CONC-1] spawn-in-loop x OWN-11 (~L52-53) vs the admission claim (~L59-63)
findings.title: OWN-11 makes runtime-count fan-out unspellable, contradicting the delta's own admission rationale
findings.program_or_defect: Inside loop @l a borrow_expr may only name regions introduced inside @l's body (OWN-11, and the draft's own interaction note concedes it), so `scope 'p { loop @l { spawn['p](worker, &'p shared); } }` rejects — a scope can spawn only a compile-time-fixed straight-line number of borrow-capturing children. The admission cites 'every concurrent 51-map scenario (11, 38, 41, 42, 44)'; N-worker data-parallel scenarios need loop-spawn, so the drafted rules reject the named consumers the D16 bar requires.
findings.minimal_fix: Either add a marked OWN-11 carve-out for spawn captures at the enclosing scope region (sound because the scope-local pseudo-entries from the FATAL-1 fix enforce cross-iteration disjointness and the join precedes 'p's end), or shrink the admission claim to the fixed-fan-out scenarios it actually covers.

findings.surface: [CONC-2]/CQ-1/CQ-3 Sendable mechanism (optables.md L195, 201)
findings.title: S.3 says 'T must conform Sendable' (FN-3 contract machinery) while CONC-2 derives it structurally — two mechanisms, neither pinned
findings.program_or_defect: FN-3 conformance is user-declared, at most one per (type, contract), with members; CONC-2's judgment is built-in and structural. If Sendable is a user-declarable contract, a writer can conform a non-Sendable type (a forgery channel); if built-in, CQ-1/CQ-3's 'conform' wording is wrong rule text.
findings.minimal_fix: One sentence in CONC-2: Sendable/Shareable are built-in structural predicates (the Int/Float closed-conformer precedent), never user-conformable; amend CQ-1/CQ-3 wording in the same landing.

findings.surface: [CONC-V2-6] Walk 1 spelling (~L347-370)
findings.title: spawn has no targ position, so Walk 1's brand instantiation ''q := 'qa' is checked by hand-waving; SpawnError is undefined; capture-list arg convention (GRAM-11 named vs positional) is unstated
findings.program_or_defect: `spawn['p](producer, move t)` gives no place to write the explicit instantiation TYPE-5 mandates (compare derivation 1's `produce<B>(tx: move t, ...)`); the walk asserts the body 'instantiates 'q := 'qa' with no spelling. spawn returns own Result<unit, SpawnError> but SpawnError appears in no prelude or companion-enum block. GRAM-11 requires named args at user fns; spawn passes positional captures to a named fn with no rule saying which convention governs.
findings.minimal_fix: Give spawn a grammar line: spawn's targ list carries the body's explicit type/region/brand args, captures are named per GRAM-11; add `enum SpawnError { Eagain(); }` to the companion enums.

findings.surface: [CONC-V2-6] Walk 2 &-capture ground (~L386-388)
findings.title: Walk 2 mis-cites R6 for rejecting &'p g — R6 governs the loaned place (the mutex interior), not borrows of the holder g; the real ground is R3
findings.program_or_defect: 'independently R6 forbids it — the guard holds a live interior-uniq entry, so &-sharing it would hand a second thread the exclusive interior' — R6's prohibitions attach to P = m.interior and places overlapping it; g's own binding place does not overlap P, so no R6 clause fires on `&'p g`. What actually rejects is R3: a borrow minted from a live confined binding is statement-scoped and consumed by the receiving call, so a 'p-persistent capture borrow of g is unspellable (plus the CONC-2 never-Shareable stipulation).
findings.minimal_fix: Rewrite the clause citation: R3 statement-scoped-mint (plus CONC-2 loan-token row); drop the R6 claim.

findings.surface: [CONC-V2-6] Walk 3 x ratified R14 (~L334-338, 391-412)
findings.title: CONC silently amends ratified R14 — 'a replicate & slot requires the referent Shareable' is a new reject premise on a ratified rule with no amendment marker
findings.program_or_defect: RULES-RATIFIED R14 (with AMD-1) checks loan disjointness only; CONC-V2-6 adds a capability premise to every par slot ('Both must pass'). The repo's discipline marks every ratified-rule change [AMD-n]; this one is unmarked and uncounted, and Walk 3 Direction B's reject depends entirely on it.
findings.minimal_fix: Carry it as a marked amendment (AMD-7: par slot capability premise — replicate requires referent Shareable, split-unique requires Sendable-and-exclusively-transferred) and count it as a rule change.

findings.surface: Delta-1 rule count and D2 spec-mass budget (whole delta, ~L19-412)
findings.title: Honest kernel-rule count is ~11, not the implied 4, and verbatim adoption straddles or busts the 48k always-loaded budget
findings.program_or_defect: Corrected honest count: (1) scope statement/region, (2) spawn capture legality, (3) capture-entry persistence + scope-local disjointness [FATAL-1 fix], (4) exit-edge join [FATAL-2 fix], (5) scope effect row, (6) Sendable class judgment, (7) Shareable + structural recursion incl. user composites, (8) boundary capability premise (the R14 amendment), (9) guard classification + the new interior-view loan clause [FATAL-3 fix], (10) trap-abort composition (CONC-4), (11) the release/acquire publication axiom the draft itself flags as un-axiomatized — plus the full mutex form-table section (table data, but counted spec mass per the no-shadow-spec rule). Budget: always-loaded set today = kernel-spec 57.4kB + optables 39.9kB + cards 43.9kB + handout 8.1kB = 149.2kB ≈ 37-43k tokens at 3.5-4 bytes/token, i.e. ~78-90% of the ratified ~48k before ANY delta. Delta 1 verbatim is 25.9kB ≈ 6.5-7.4k tokens → 44-50k (no headroom, likely over); all six deltas verbatim (72.8kB ≈ 18-21k) → ~55-64k, clearly over. The draft never states a token count.
findings.minimal_fix: Land a normative-only cut of Delta 1 (rule text + form rows, rationale and regression walks moved to the review record — roughly a 60-70% mass reduction), publish a measured token count of the resulting always-loaded set against 48k, and restate the delta as ~11 rules + 1 form section.

lens: FIDELITY AND KERNEL INTERACTION — does the CONC delta's text say what its rationale claims, and does it compose with CAP-1, R1-R15 (esp. R14/R15/AMD-1/AMD-5/AMD-6), the region rules (OWN-3/4/6/10/11), R7/STOR-3 drops, GRAM-9 ANF, EFF-1/EFF-2, and the D2 spec-mass budget
verdict: REJECT AS DRAFTED. The architecture's shape is right — capability read off AMD-6 declaration classes, R15 as the single named cross-thread trust point, trap=whole-process-abort composing cleanly with R7 — and it repelled every direct forgery I could spell (see hardest_failed). But three holes are FATAL under the delta's own cited kernel rules: (1) spawn capture borrows are OWN-6 statement-scoped temporaries and AMD-1 is statement-local, so two spawns or spawn-then-parent-write alias a &uniq across threads — the exact data race D1 declares impossible; (2) return/break out of a scope block is never required to join, giving a child a &'p borrow into a popped frame; (3) guard_uniq has no loan clause, its statement-scoped story is unspellable under GRAM-9 ANF, and the only ratified clause spelling (issues uniq on source(receiver)) is AMD-5 declaration-rejected — 'no new loan machinery' is false and two live &uniq interior views are spellable. Beneath those, CONC-2 does bind CAP-1's words but does not actually cover the type system: no rows for user structs/enums/array/Option/Result, a vacuous 'deeply immutable' Shareable predicate, an ill-founded recursion through box, and a class partition that files user confined types as 'ordinary owned'. The regression appendix is internally inconsistent: Walk 1's capture-as-argument model contradicts Walk 2's capture-as-env-store firing ground, and Walk 2 mis-cites R6. Honest rule count is ~11 kernel rules plus a full mutex form-table section and a publication-order axiom, not 4; the always-loaded set is already at ~78-90% of the 48k budget and verbatim adoption busts it. Every fix above is local and one-clause-to-one-rule sized; the delta is repairable, but nothing in it should be transcribed to spec before a re-attack on the repaired text — exactly as its own adoption note demands.
hardest_failed: Move a locked mutex into a spawn (own-capture m while a guard is live): fails — the guard's uniq entry on m.interior overlaps m, and R6's first sentence forbids moving any overlapping place, before CONC-2 is even consulted.

Cross-thread brand launder: feed a spawn body fn f[brand 'q](tx: cq_tx<'q,u64>, rx: cq_rx<'q,u64>) endpoints of two different queues — rejected at the call by TYPE-5 nominal brand-constituent equality plus the 5.4 all-carriers tie; spawn adds no bypass since the capture rides the ordinary CALL check (CONC-V2-1).

spsc clone-in-child ep forge: pass an spsc-minted endpoint into an mpmc-bounded brand-generic body that clones — rejected at instantiation by clause (ii) recorded-bound tag equality (CQ-3/TAG-1); erasure leaves nothing to forge at runtime.

Guard heap/container smuggle to another thread (box<guard>, seq<guard,N>, a user wrapper struct): every spelling is ill-formed at R1 (confined type as generic/sealed-form instantiation, borrow/confined fields in non-confined aggregates) before any CONC rule is needed.

Observe a skipped teardown after a sibling-thread trap (half-unlocked mutex, undrained queue read by a survivor): fails — CONC-4's whole-process abort leaves no in-process observer, so points (1)-(4) hold, conditional only on the TCB fact that the abort is genuinely process-wide (correctly parked under the R15/H4 review).
